clean_output:
article_id: art-2026-03-04-003
summary: The article keeps the main bounds visible: low measured adoption is real within the studied tool set, and AGENTS.md looks broadly stable overall but more instruction-active in the high-churn subset.
review_confidence: high
contradictions:
  - contradiction_text: The headline 5% adoption figure can be read as general AI configuration scarcity, but the measurement covers only four commercial tool formats and excludes other ecosystems.
    related_claim_texts:
      - Only 466 scanned repositories, or 5%, had adopted at least one of the AI configuration file formats examined in the study.
    related_fact_refs:
      - fact-001
      - fact-002
    related_evidence_ids:
      - evidence-01
      - evidence-02
    disposition: bounded
    disclosure: Keep visible that the 5% figure measures adoption only within the study's defined tool set, not all AI configuration practice.
    confidence: high
  - contradiction_text: AGENTS.md appears mostly stable after creation at the file-history level, yet the detailed evolution sample shows repeated instruction edits in the most active files.
    related_claim_texts:
      - AGENTS.md files in the study were often stable after creation, but when change occurred in the detailed high-activity sample it was usually focused on adding or modifying instructions rather than broad multi-category revisions.
    related_fact_refs:
      - fact-009
      - fact-010
      - fact-011
      - fact-012
    related_evidence_ids:
      - evidence-09
      - evidence-10
      - evidence-11
      - evidence-12
    disposition: bounded
    disclosure: Keep visible that both patterns are true for different slices of the sample: broad file-level histories show low churn, while the commit-category analysis is restricted to a high-activity subset of 10 files.
